card.intuition: Copying data from one database server (the leader) to one or more others (followers) to improve reliability and read performance.
card.analogy: A CEO sending a memo to 10 department heads. The CEO makes the 'write' (creates the memo), and then 10 people can 'read' it simultaneously in their own offices.
when_to_use: Improving read throughput (Leader handles writes, Followers handle reads)
High Availability (if the Leader dies, a Follower can be promoted to Leader)
Backups and reporting (running heavy reports on a follower so it doesn't slow down the main app)
gotchas: Replication Lag: if a user writes a post and instantly refreshes, they might hit a follower that hasn't received the update yet
Synchronous replication is slower (Leader waits for Followers) but has zero lag; Asynchronous is faster but risks data loss during a crash

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
App routes writes to primary, reads to replicas.
Primary commits write and appends to Write-Ahead Log.
Replicas stream and apply log entries — slight replication lag.

exercise.prompt: Decide which database instance to use for a specific operation.
hints: All writes/modifications must go to the 'Leader' (or Master)
Reads can be distributed to 'Follower' (or Slave/Replica) nodes
